title: Portable sterilization and decontamination system
abstract: A portable sterilization and decontamination system is described. The system includes a fuel cell configured to generate electricity and at least one of water or water vapor and a heating system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the heating system to convert the energy to heat and provide the heat to a determined volume. The system further includes a humidifying system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the humidifying system to utilize at least one of the electricity or the at least one of water or water vapor to produce moisture and provide the moisture to the determined volume and a control system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the heating system and the humidifying system, the control system to monitor and control the fuel cell, the heating system and the humidifying system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A portable sterilization and decontamination system, comprising: a fuel cell configured to generate electricity and at least one of water or water vapor; a heating system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the heating system to convert the electricity to heat and provide the heat to a determined volume; a humidifying system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the humidifying system to utilize at least one of the electricity or the at least one of water or water vapor to produce moisture and provide the moisture to the determined volume; and a control system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the heating system and the humidifying system, the control system to monitor and control the fuel cell, the heating system and the humidifying system. 2. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, further comprising: one or more temperature sensors operatively coupled to the control system, the one or more temperature sensors being configured to measure a temperature associated with the determined volume; and one or more humidity sensors operatively coupled to the control system, the one or more humidity sensors being configured to measure a humidity associated with the determined volume. 3. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, further comprising a lighting system operatively coupled to the fuel cell and the control system, the lighting system to convert the electricity to ultraviolet light and provide the ultraviolet light to the determined volume. 4. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, wherein the heating system comprises an induction heater to generate the heat by electromagnetic induction. 5. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, wherein the heating system comprises an electric resistance heater. 6. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, further comprising: a fan system operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the fan system being to move heated air produced by the fuel cell. 7. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, further comprising: a reformer operatively coupled to the fuel cell, the reformer to extract hydrogen from hydrocarbon fuel and provide the extracted hydrogen to the fuel cell. 8. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, further comprising: a telematics system operatively coupled to the control system, the telematics system to transmit and receive parameters associated with the portable sterilization and decontamination. 9. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, wherein the determined volume comprises an insulated chamber. 10. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, wherein the determined volume comprises a surface of an interior space. 11. The portable sterilization and decontamination system of claim 1, wherein at least a portion of the portable sterilization and decontamination system is coupled to a portable chassis. 12. A method, comprising: providing electricity for a portable sterilization system using a fuel cell; applying heat to a determined volume using a heating system operatively coupled to the fuel cell; and applying moisture to the determined volume using a humidifying system operatively coupled to the fuel cell. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: applying ultraviolet light to the determined volume using a lighting system operatively coupled to the fuel cell. 14. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, by a control system of the portable sterilization system, a temperature associated with the determined volume; 

 determining, by the control system, whether the temperature associated with the determined volume satisfies a temperature threshold; and in response to determining that the temperature satisfies the temperature threshold, adjusting heat output of the heating system. 15. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, by a control system of the portable sterilization system, a humidity associated with the determined volume; determining, by the control system, whether the humidity associated with the determined volume satisfies a humidity threshold; and in response to determining that the humidity satisfies the humidity threshold, adjusting moisture output of the humidity system. 16. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, by a control system of the portable sterilization system, an adjustment to at least one of the heat or the moisture; and adjusting, by the control system, at least one of heat output or moisture output of the portable sterilization system based on the received adjustment. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the adjusting of the at least one of the heat output or the moisture output of the portable sterilization system is based on a psychometric chart. 18. The method of claim 12, further comprising: transmitting, by a control system via a telematics system, one or more parameters associated with the portable sterilization system to a client device; receiving, from the client device, an adjustment to the one or more parameters associated with the portable sterilization system; and adjusting the one or more parameters associated with the portable sterilization system based on the received adjustment. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein transmitting the one or more parameters associated with the portable sterilization system to a client device further comprises: generating a user interface for presentation on the client device, the user interface comprising information associated with the one or more parameters associated with the portable sterilization system. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the user interface comprises a psychometric chart.
